summary_one_line: Input must be a GDEF table whose version.major equals 1 or 2 so has_glyph_classes() avoids the default false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A GDEF table header whose 16-bit major-version field decodes to 1 or 2 (bytes 00 01 at the version offset) together with the 'GDEF' tag, so the switch in has_glyph_classes() dispatches to case 1 or case 2 instead of the default arm.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins 00 01 00 00 ... 47 44 45 46 ('GDEF'), giving version.major=1, so the source switch reaches case 1 (line 680, T=399) or case 2 (line 682) and the blocker default branch is taken False (W: line 684 F=468, never True).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry no valid GDEF version header (e.g. 87 0e 0d 0e, fe ff ff ff, e0 17 00 00) so version.major is neither 1 nor 2; the switch falls through to default: return false, taking the blocker True side (L: line 684 T=440).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operands from the version-major comparison and the 'GDEF' tag memcmp, then substitutes the exact constant (01 for major, the GDEF FOURCC) into the input at the matching offsets; naive/value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot guess these exact multi-byte magic constants by havoc or Hamming-distance feedback alone.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of a switch on u.version.major in has_glyph_classes(); the winning side requires major 1 or 2.
Case 1 reaches when major version is 1.
The blocked (loser) side is the default false return.
Winners take case 1/case 2 while losers take the default branch, per per-role branch counts.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 684  T=0 F=468  T=440 F=0  default: return false;  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds begin with a GDEF version header 00 01 00 00 and the GDEF tag.
The four bytes spelling GDEF are constant across all winner seeds and absent in losers (DIFFER tag at those offsets).
A loser-blocking seed carries no GDEF version header.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes the matching CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive and value_profile have no I2SRandReplace.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5222 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 13b375b9eff5c760` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 10 and 11 of the 12-level ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness switching on a 16-bit version field where naive/value_profile resolve the case-1/case-2 arms as fast as cmplog/vpc, showing the exact magic constant is reachable without I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The 'GDEF' tag and the version-major byte come from different comparison sites, so I cannot fully separate which exact CMP operand I2S substituted to flip the switch versus the tag match.
